runs.context_blueprint: {"language": "R", "build_system": "make", "package_manager": "R", "build_commands": ["R CMD INSTALL --no-multiarch --with-keep.source ."], "install_commands": ["apt-get update", "apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends build-essential libcurl4-openssl-dev libssl-dev libxml2-dev libgit2-dev", "rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*"], "runtime_requirements": {"language_version": "4.5.3", "system_packages": ["build-essential", "libcurl4-openssl-dev", "libssl-dev", "libxml2-dev", "libgit2-dev"]}, "repo_type": "library", "base_image": "r-base:4.5.3", "base_image_rationale": "Official R base image with R 4.5.3 suitable for building CRAN-like R packages.", "pitfalls": ["Some CRAN dependencies may require additional system libraries not listed; you may need to install libicu-dev or libz-dev depending on optional dependencies.", "No -slim variant available for r-base in the listed official images; larger image sizes may be needed."], "notes": "This blueprint targets building the dplyr package from source within an R 4.5.3 environment. The workflow includes dependency resolution via R CMD INSTALL, which will pull CRAN dependencies as needed."}

steps.tool_input:
{"path": "Dockerfile", "content": "FROM r-base:4.5.3

ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive

# Install system dependencies required to build dplyr from source
RUN apt-get update \
  && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
    build-essential \
    libcurl4-openssl-dev \
    libssl-dev \
    libxml2-dev \
    libgit2-dev \
  && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Build and install the package from source
WORKDIR /usr/local/src/dplyr
COPY . .
RUN R CMD INSTALL --no-multiarch --with-keep.source .

# Provide a simple runtime command to verify the package is usable
CMD ["R", "-e", "library(dplyr); cat('dplyr loaded, version', packageVersion('dplyr'), '\n')"]
"}

steps.tool_input:
{"path": "Dockerfile", "content": "FROM r-base:4.5.3

ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive

# Install system dependencies required to build dplyr from source
RUN apt-get update \
  && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
    build-essential \
    libcurl4-openssl-dev \
    libssl-dev \
    libxml2-dev \
    libgit2-dev \
  && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Install R package dependencies required by dplyr before building
RUN R -e 'install.packages(c("cli","generics","glue","lifecycle","magrittr","pillar","R6","rlang","tibble","tidyselect","vctrs"), repos="https://cloud.r-project.org")'

# Build and install the package from source
WORKDIR /usr/local/src/dplyr
COPY . .
RUN R CMD INSTALL --no-multiarch --with-keep.source .

# Provide a simple runtime command to verify the package is usable
CMD ["R", "-e", "library(dplyr); cat('dplyr loaded, version', packageVersion('dplyr'), '\n')"]
"}

steps.tool_input:
{"path": "Dockerfile", "content": "FROM r-base:4.5.3

ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive

# Install system dependencies required to build dplyr from source
RUN apt-get update \
  && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
    build-essential \
    libcurl4-openssl-dev \
    libssl-dev \
    libxml2-dev \
    libgit2-dev \
  && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Install R package build-time dependencies
RUN R -e 'install.packages(c("Rcpp","cli","generics","glue","lifecycle","magrittr","pillar","R6","rlang","tibble","tidyselect","vctrs"), repos="https://cloud.r-project.org")'

# Build and install the package from source
WORKDIR /usr/local/src/dplyr
COPY . .
RUN R CMD INSTALL --no-multiarch --with-keep.source .

# Provide a simple runtime command to verify the package is usable
CMD ["bash","-lc","R -e \"library(dplyr); cat('dplyr loaded, version ', packageVersion('dplyr'), '\\n')\""]
"}

steps.tool_input:
{"path": "Dockerfile", "content": "FROM r-base:4.5.3

ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive

# Install system dependencies required to build dplyr from source
RUN apt-get update \
  && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
    build-essential \
    libcurl4-openssl-dev \
    libssl-dev \
    libxml2-dev \
    libgit2-dev \
    libicu-dev \
  && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Install R package build-time dependencies (and Rcpp for compilation)
RUN R -e 'install.packages(c("Rcpp","cli","generics","glue","lifecycle","magrittr","pillar","R6","rlang","tibble","tidyselect","vctrs"), repos="https://cloud.r-project.org")'

# Build and install the package from source
WORKDIR /usr/local/src/dplyr
COPY . .
RUN R CMD INSTALL --no-multiarch --with-keep.source .

# Provide a simple runtime command to verify the package is usable
CMD ["R","-e","library(dplyr); cat('dplyr loaded, version ', packageVersion('dplyr'), '\n')"]
"}

steps.tool_input:
{"path": "Dockerfile", "content": "FROM r-base:4.5.3

ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive

# Install system dependencies required to build dplyr from source
RUN apt-get update \
  && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
    build-essential \
    libcurl4-openssl-dev \
    libssl-dev \
    libxml2-dev \
    libgit2-dev \
    libicu-dev \
  && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Install R package build-time dependencies (and Rcpp for compilation)
RUN R -e 'install.packages(c("Rcpp","cli","generics","glue","lifecycle","magrittr","pillar","R6","rlang","tibble","tidyselect","vctrs"), repos="https://cloud.r-project.org")'

# Build and install the package from source
WORKDIR /usr/local/src/dplyr
COPY . .
RUN R CMD INSTALL --no-multiarch --with-keep.source .

# Create a small helper script to verify the installation at runtime
RUN printf '#!/bin/bash\nset -e\nRscript -e "library(dplyr); cat(\"dplyr loaded, version \", packageVersion(\'dplyr\'), \"\\n\")"' > /usr/local/bin/check-dplyr.sh \
  && chmod +x /usr/local/bin/check-dplyr.sh

# Runtime entrypoint: run a quick check to ensure dplyr is usable
CMD ["/usr/local/bin/check-dplyr.sh"]
"}

steps.tool_input:
{"path": "Dockerfile", "content": "FROM r-base:4.5.3

ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive

# Install system dependencies required to build dplyr from source
RUN apt-get update \
  && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
    build-essential \
    libcurl4-openssl-dev \
    libssl-dev \
    libxml2-dev \
    libgit2-dev \
    libicu-dev \
  && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Install R package build-time dependencies (and Rcpp for compilation)
RUN R -e 'install.packages(c("Rcpp","cli","generics","glue","lifecycle","magrittr","pillar","R6","rlang","tibble","tidyselect","vctrs"), repos="https://cloud.r-project.org")'

# Build and install the package from source
WORKDIR /usr/local/src/dplyr
COPY . .
RUN R CMD INSTALL --no-multiarch --with-keep.source .

# Create a small helper script to verify the installation at runtime
RUN bash -lc 'cat > /usr/local/bin/check-dplyr.sh <<"EOS"
#!/bin/bash
set -e
Rscript -e "library(dplyr); cat(\"dplyr loaded, version \\", packageVersion(\'dplyr\'), \\"}

iterations.injected_lessons:
1. **Base image issues**  
   - Tried `FROM r-base:4.5.3`. Multiple builds failed with “tag may not exist” or “unavailable in registry.”  
   - **Action:** Use a verified base image tag (e.g., `r-base:4.3.2` or latest valid tag from Docker Hub). Confirm availability with `docker pull r-base:<tag>` before building.

2. **Missing system and R dependencies**  
   - Fails mentioned missing `Rcpp` (a dependency of `dplyr`) and possibly other build tools required by R packages.  
   - **Action:** In the Dockerfile, `RUN apt-get install -y libcurl4-openssl-dev libssl-dev libxml2-dev` and pre-install R packages like `Rcpp` before installing `dplyr`.

3. **Invalid package install command**  
   - Error: `Warning: invalid package '.'` and `ERROR: no packages specified` occurred when `R CMD INSTALL .` was run. This indicates the current directory did not contain a valid R package (missing `DESCRIPTION`).  
   - **Action:** Either copy a valid package source directory with a `DESCRIPTION` file into the image, or explicitly install `dplyr` from CRAN using  
     ```bash
     RUN R -e "install.packages('dplyr', repos='https://cloud.r-project.org')"
     ```  
     instead of `R CMD INSTALL .`.

4. **COPY and context problems**  
   - Builds failed or copied too much/unnecessary content with `COPY . .`.  
   - **Action:** Use a `.dockerignore` to exclude non-essential files (git, data, tests, vignettes, pkgdown).  
     Copy only what’s needed for installation:  
     ```dockerfile
     COPY DESCRIPTION NAMESPACE R/ /usr/local/src/app/
     WORKDIR /usr/local/src/app
     ```

5. **Smoke test syntax error**  
   - Final smoke test failed: `sh: 1: Syntax error: Unterminated quoted string`  
   - **Action:** Escape quotes correctly, e.g.  
     ```bash
     RUN R -e "library(dplyr); cat('dplyr version:', packageVersion('dplyr'), '\n')"
     ```

6. **Build script flow problems**  
   - Multiple steps repeated the same `apt-get` chain but never cleaned up caches.  
   - **Action:** Chain commands with `&&` and finish with `rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*` to keep image lean.

7. **Verification improvements**  
   - Previous builds only tested library load.  
   - **Action:** Add a quick R command verifying a basic function:  
     ```bash
     RUN R -e "library(dplyr); stopifnot('data.frame' %in% class(mtcars %>% summarise(avg=mean(mpg))))"
     ```  

Use these corrections to ensure the next Dockerfile builds successfully and passes basic functional checks.

steps.tool_input:
{"path": "Dockerfile", "content": "FROM r-base:4.5.3

LABEL maintainer="tidyverse-build-agent"

ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive

RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
  build-essential \
  libcurl4-openssl-dev \
  libssl-dev \
  libxml2-dev \
  libgit2-dev \
  libicu-dev \
  ca-certificates \
  && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

WORKDIR /usr/local/src/dplyr

COPY . /usr/local/src/dplyr

RUN R CMD INSTALL --no-multiarch --with-keep.source .

CMD ["R","-e","library(dplyr); cat('dplyr version:', as.character(packageVersion('dplyr')), '\\n')"]
"}

{"path": "Dockerfile", "content": "FROM r-base:4.5.3

LABEL maintainer="tidyverse-build-agent"

ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive

RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
  build-essential \
  libcurl4-openssl-dev \
  libssl-dev \
  libxml2-dev \
  libgit2-dev \
  ca-certificates \
  && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

WORKDIR /usr/local/src/dplyr

COPY . /usr/local/src/dplyr

# Pre-install common CRAN dependencies to satisfy R CMD INSTALL
RUN R -e "install.packages(c('cli','generics','glue','lifecycle','magrittr','pillar','R6','rlang','tibble','tidyselect','vctrs'), repos='https://cloud.r-project.org')"

RUN R CMD INSTALL --no-multiarch --with-keep.source .

CMD ["R","-e","library(dplyr); cat('dplyr version:', as.character(packageVersion('dplyr')), '\\n')"]
"}

{"path": "Dockerfile", "content": "FROM r-base:4.5.3

LABEL maintainer="tidyverse-build-agent"

ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive

RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
  build-essential \
  libcurl4-openssl-dev \
  libssl-dev \
  libxml2-dev \
  libgit2-dev \
  ca-certificates \
  && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

WORKDIR /usr/local/src/dplyr

COPY . /usr/local/src/dplyr

# Pre-install common CRAN dependencies to satisfy R CMD INSTALL
RUN R -e "install.packages(c('cli','generics','glue','lifecycle','magrittr','pillar','R6','rlang','tibble','tidyselect','vctrs'), repos='https://cloud.r-project.org')"

# Create a small R script to verify the build
RUN bash -lc 'cat > /usr/local/src/dplyr/check_version.R << "EOF"
library(dplyr)
cat("dplyr version:", as.character(packageVersion("dplyr")), "\n")
EOF
'

CMD ["Rscript","/usr/local/src/dplyr/check_version.R"]
"}

LABEL maintainer="tidyverse-build-agent"

ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive

RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
  build-essential \
  libcurl4-openssl-dev \

  libxml2-dev \
  libgit2-dev \
  ca-certificates \
  && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

WORKDIR /usr/local/src/dplyr

COPY . /usr/local/src/dplyr

# Pre-install common CRAN dependencies to satisfy R CMD INSTALL

'

RUN Rscript /usr/local/src/dplyr/check_version.R

CMD ["Rscript","/usr/local/src/dplyr/check_version.R"]
"}

{"path": "Dockerfile", "content": "FROM r-base:4.3.2

LABEL maintainer="tidyverse-build-agent"

ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive

RUN set -eux; \
  apt-get update; \
  apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
    build-essential \
    libcurl4-openssl-dev \
    libssl-dev \
    libxml2-dev \
    libgit2-dev \
    ca-certificates \
    libicu-dev \
  ; \
  rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

WORKDIR /usr/local/src/dplyr

COPY . .

RUN R -e "install.packages(c('cli','generics','glue','lifecycle','magrittr','pillar','R6','rlang','tibble','tidyselect','vctrs'), repos='https://cloud.r-project.org')"

RUN R CMD INSTALL --no-multiarch --with-keep.source .

CMD ["R","-e","library(dplyr); cat('dplyr version:', as.character(packageVersion('dplyr')), '\\n')"]
"}

verify_build_details.build_error:
Summary of Docker build error

- Failing command/step:
  - [6/6] RUN R CMD INSTALL --no-multiarch --with-keep.source .

- Exact error message and exit code:
  - Final failure: "ERROR: dependencies ‘cli’, ‘lifecycle’, ‘pillar’, ‘rlang’, ‘tibble’, ‘tidyselect’, ‘vctrs’ are not available for package ‘dplyr’"
  - Exit code: 1 (the step ended non-zero and the overall build failed with: process "/bin/sh -c R CMD INSTALL --no-multiarch --with-keep.source ." did not complete successfully: exit code: 1)

- The failing command/step (context):
  - In Dockerfile, after earlier steps, the build reached:
    - RUN R CMD INSTALL --no-multiarch --with-keep.source .
  - This step attempted to install dplyr (and its dependencies) from source but failed due to missing dependencies.

- Missing packages or files mentioned:
  - Primary missing dependencies for dplyr: cli, lifecycle, pillar, rlang, tibble, tidyselect, vctrs (as reported by the final error).
  - Earlier package build failures that contributed to the cascade:
    - "ERROR: compilation failed for package ‘utf8’" (during installation of cli/generics/glue/etc.)
    - "ERROR: compilation failed for package ‘cli’" (also seen during the same phase)
    - Assembly errors such as "unknown pseudo-op: `.base64`" in multiple assembler outputs (affecting utf8, rlang, etc.)

- Version mismatch / environment details (what to note):
  - Base image is using R 4.3.2: R version 4.3.2 (2023-10-31) – "Eye Holes".
  - The apt-get install step shows extensive Debian package upgrades (53 upgraded, 68 newly installed, 8 to remove, 199 not upgraded), pulling in newer GCC/libc/OpenSSL/etc from Debian testing/sid, which can cause ABI/toolchain mismatches with the R base image and CRAN packages.
  - The log includes mixed package sources ( Debian sid/testing, and the r-base image), indicating a toolchain/library version mismatch that likely contributed to the build failures for utf8/cli/rlang and downstream dependencies.

- Notable version-related lines:
  - R version: 4.3.2 (2023-10-31)
  - APT upgrade summary: "53 upgraded, 68 newly installed, 8 to remove and 199 not upgraded." (indicating a non-stable mix of libraries)
  - Early-stage failures show assembler-based compilation issues for utf8/cli/rlang, consistent with a toolchain mismatch.

In short: The final build failure stems from missing CRAN package dependencies for dplyr (cli, lifecycle, pillar, rlang, tibble, tidyselect, vctrs) caused by prior compilation failures (utf8/cli) likely due to a mismatched or unstable system toolchain and libraries introduced during the Debian apt-get upgrade in the image. The exact blocker is the R CMD INSTALL step failing with exit code 1 due to those unresolved dependencies.

steps.tool_input:
{"path": "Dockerfile", "content": "FROM r-base:4.5.3

LABEL maintainer="tidyverse-build-agent"

ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive

RUN set -eux; \
  apt-get update; \
  apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
    build-essential \
    libcurl4-openssl-dev \
    libssl-dev \
    libxml2-dev \
    libgit2-dev \
    libicu-dev \
    ca-certificates \
  ; \
  rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

WORKDIR /usr/local/src/dplyr

COPY . /usr/local/src/dplyr

# Pre-install common CRAN dependencies to satisfy R CMD INSTALL
RUN R -e "install.packages(c('cli','generics','glue','lifecycle','magrittr','pillar','R6','rlang','tibble','tidyselect','vctrs'), repos='https://cloud.r-project.org')"

# Build dplyr from source
RUN R CMD INSTALL --no-multiarch --with-keep.source .

CMD ["R","-e","library(dplyr); cat('dplyr version:', as.character(packageVersion('dplyr')), '\\n')"]
"}

{"path": "Dockerfile", "content": "FROM r-base:4.5.3

LABEL maintainer="tidyverse-build-agent"

ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive

RUN set -eux; \
  apt-get update; \
  apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
    build-essential \
    libcurl4-openssl-dev \
    libssl-dev \
    libxml2-dev \
    libgit2-dev \
    libicu-dev \
    ca-certificates \
  ; \
  rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

WORKDIR /usr/local/src/dplyr

COPY . /usr/local/src/dplyr

# Pre-install common CRAN dependencies to satisfy R CMD INSTALL
RUN R -e "install.packages(c('cli','generics','glue','lifecycle','magrittr','pillar','R6','rlang','tibble','tidyselect','vctrs'), repos='https://cloud.r-project.org')"

# Build dplyr from source
RUN R CMD INSTALL --no-multiarch --with-keep.source .

# Create a small R script to verify the build
RUN bash -lc 'cat > /usr/local/src/dplyr/check_version.R << "EOF"
library(dplyr)
cat("dplyr version:", as.character(packageVersion("dplyr")), "\n")
EOF
'

# Smoke test script execution
CMD ["Rscript", "/usr/local/src/dplyr/check_version.R"]
"}

steps.tool_input:
{"path": "Dockerfile", "content": "FROM r-base:4.3.2

LABEL maintainer="tidyverse-build-agent"

ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive

RUN set -eux; \
  apt-get update; \
  apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
    build-essential \
    libcurl4-openssl-dev \
    libssl-dev \
    libxml2-dev \
    libgit2-dev \
    ca-certificates \
  ; \
  rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

WORKDIR /usr/local/src/dplyr

COPY . /usr/local/src/dplyr

# Pre-install common CRAN dependencies to satisfy R CMD INSTALL
RUN R -e "install.packages(c('cli','generics','glue','lifecycle','magrittr','pillar','R6','rlang','tibble','tidyselect','vctrs'), repos='https://cloud.r-project.org')"

# Build dplyr from source
RUN R CMD INSTALL --no-multiarch --with-keep.source .

# Smoke test: verify the installed package version
RUN R -e "library(dplyr); cat('dplyr version:', as.character(packageVersion('dplyr')), '\n')"

CMD ["R","-e","library(dplyr); cat('ready\n')"]
"}

- The cascade ends with the final failure above at step [6/7] leading to the docker build failing at step 10.

Missing packages or files mentioned

- Root cause visible in logs: “ERROR: compilation failed for package ‘utf8’” with assembler errors (unknown pseudo-op .base64) blocking initial dependencies (utf8, cli).

Version and mismatch information
- Base image: r-base:4.3.2 (R version 4.3.2, 2023-10-31) — “R version 4.3.2 (2023-10-31) -- "Eye Holes"”
- Evidence of version/branch mismatch during build: apt-get install step pulls many Debian sid/testing packages (e.g., gcc-16-base, libc6 2.42-13, openssl 3.5.5-1, etc.), indicating mixing unstable/bleeding-edge packages with the R base image.
- This combination coincides with the assembler errors and subsequent R package compilation failures.

Summary
- Immediate cause: R package installation of dplyr fails because dependencies (cli, lifecycle, pillar, rlang, tibble, tidyselect, vctrs) are not available, themselves caused by prior compilation failures (notably utf8) with assembler errors (unknown pseudo-op .base64).
- Actionable next step: avoid mixing Debian sid/testing packages with the R base image, or pin/align package sources to stable releases, or use prebuilt binary wheels for R packages instead of compiling from source in this environment.

iterations.injected_lessons:
1. **Base image problems:**  
   - Attempts used `FROM r-base:4.5.3` and `FROM r-base:4.3.2`.  
   - `r-base:4.5.3` led to `smoke_failed` and syntax errors (`"(" unexpected`), suggesting that tag wasn’t available or image shell misconfigured.  
   - `r-base:4.3.2` is valid on Docker Hub and should be kept.  
   **Next:** Use only a verified tag (`r-base:4.3.2` or `r-base:latest`). Avoid 4.5.x until confirmed built upstream.

2. **Missing R package dependencies:**  
   - Failure at `RUN R CMD INSTALL --no-multiarch --with-keep.source .`  
   - Error: `ERROR: dependencies ‘cli’ ...` (and likely other tidyverse deps) not available.  
   **Next:** Before `R CMD INSTALL`, pre-install dependencies via:  
     ```bash
     R -e "install.packages(c('devtools','remotes','cli','rlang','vctrs','glue','pillar','tibble','pkgbuild','testthat'), repos='https://cloud.r-project.org')"
     ```  
     Or use `Rscript -e "remotes::install_deps(dependencies=TRUE)"` if `remotes` is installed.

3. **System library gaps:**  
   - Unknown exact missing system packages, but tidyverse builds usually need:  
     `libcurl4-openssl-dev`, `libssl-dev`, `libxml2-dev`, `libfontconfig1-dev`, `libharfbuzz-dev`, `libfribidi-dev`, `libfreetype6-dev`.  
   - Earlier Dockerfiles installed only minimal apt packages.  
   **Next:** Explicitly add those packages in one `apt-get install` layer.

4. **Shell / quoting issues in smoke tests:**  
   - Smoke tests like `R -e \"library(dplyr); print('dplyr loaded')\"` failed with `Syntax error: "(" unexpected`.  
   - This indicates invalid quoting between Docker RUN shell and R command.  
   **Next:** Use single quotes for `RUN R -e 'expression'` rather than escaping inner quotes:
     ```dockerfile
     RUN R -e 'library(dplyr); print("dplyr loaded")'
     ```
     Verify correct shell quoting.

5. **Build step ordering:**  
   - The sequence lacked `WORKDIR` and a clean copy of the source before build.  
   **Next:**  
     - Add `WORKDIR /usr/src/app`  
     - `COPY . .`  
     - Then run `R CMD INSTALL --no-multiarch --with-keep.source .`

6. **.dockerignore space typo:**  
   - `.dockerignore` was written as `" .dockerignore"` (extra leading space).  
   **Next:** Ensure correct filename `.dockerignore` at root (no space).

7. **General cleanup:**  
   - Combine `apt-get update && apt-get install` in one RUN layer and clean apt cache with `rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*`.  
   - Keep `ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive` near top.

Use these corrections as the foundation for the next Dockerfile generation attempt.

steps.tool_input:
{"path": "Dockerfile", "content": "FROM r-base:4.3.2

ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive

# Install system dependencies required for building dplyr and common CRAN deps
RUN apt-get update && \
    apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
      build-essential \
      libcurl4-openssl-dev \
      libssl-dev \
      libxml2-dev \
      libgit2-dev \
      libicu-dev \
      libfontconfig1-dev \
      libharfbuzz-dev \
      libfribidi-dev \
      libfreetype6-dev \
      zlib1g-dev \
      libbz2-dev \
      liblzma-dev \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

WORKDIR /usr/src/dplyr

# Copy package source
COPY . .

# Install a minimal set of R build-time dependencies
RUN R -e 'install.packages(c("devtools","remotes","cli","rlang","vctrs","glue","pillar","tibble","pkgbuild","testthat","tidyselect"), repos="https://cloud.r-project.org")'

# Build from source
RUN R CMD INSTALL --no-multiarch --with-keep.source .

# Default command: drop into a shell (library is built in)
CMD ["bash"]
"}

- ERROR: dependencies ‘cli’, ‘generics’, ‘lifecycle’, ‘pillar’, ‘rlang’, ‘tibble’, ‘tidyselect’, ‘vctrs’ are not available for package ‘dplyr’

Failing command/step
- [6/6] RUN R CMD INSTALL --no-multiarch --with-keep.source .

Missing packages or files mentioned
- Missing dependencies preventing final install: cli, generics, lifecycle, pillar, rlang, tibble, tidyselect, vctrs
- Earlier failing compilations indicate broader toolchain/assembler issues (examples from the log): Rcpp, zip, yaml, jsonlite, stringi, digest, commonmark, utf8, cli, etc. failed to compile due to assembler errors (unknown pseudo-op “.base64”), leading to those packages not being available for later steps.

Version/compatibility notes
- Base image R version: 4.3.2
- Build uses Debian testing sid with GCC 15.x toolchain (e.g., gcc-15/gcc 15.2.0)
- Symptoms suggest a toolchain/assembler incompatibility between the Debian testing toolchain and the R package sources being built (assembler errors like unknown pseudo-op .base64 across multiple packages).

Context
- The final Docker step fails after widespread package build failures, culminating in dplyr’s dependencies not being available.

steps.tool_input:
{"path": "Dockerfile", "content": "FROM r-base:4.3.2

ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive

RUN apt-get update && \
    apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
      build-essential \
      libcurl4-openssl-dev \
      libssl-dev \
      libxml2-dev \
      libgit2-dev \
      libicu-dev \
      libfontconfig1-dev \
      libharfbuzz-dev \
      libfribidi-dev \
      libfreetype6-dev \
      zlib1g-dev \
      libbz2-dev \
      liblzma-dev \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

WORKDIR /usr/src/dplyr

COPY . .

RUN R -e 'install.packages(c("devtools","remotes","cli","rlang","vctrs","glue","pillar","tibble","pkgbuild","testthat","tidyselect"), repos="https://cloud.r-project.org")'

RUN R CMD INSTALL --no-multiarch --with-keep.source .

CMD ["bash"]
"}

{"path": "Dockerfile", "content": "FROM r-base:4.3.2

ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive

RUN apt-get update && \
    apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
      build-essential \
      libcurl4-openssl-dev \
      libssl-dev \
      libxml2-dev \
      libgit2-dev \
      libicu-dev \
      libfontconfig1-dev \
      libharfbuzz-dev \
      libfribidi-dev \
      libfreetype6-dev \
      zlib1g-dev \
      libbz2-dev \
      liblzma-dev \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

WORKDIR /usr/src/dplyr

COPY . .

# Install essential R build tools and then pre-install dependencies
RUN R -e 'install.packages(c("devtools","remotes"), repos="https://cloud.r-project.org")'
RUN R -e 'remotes::install_deps(dependencies = TRUE)'

# Build from source
RUN R CMD INSTALL --no-multiarch --with-keep.source .

CMD ["bash"]
"}

  - Assembler-related errors indicating a toolchain issue:
    - Examples: “Assembler messages: unknown pseudo-op: `.base64'” reported repeatedly during compilation of multiple packages (e.g., Rcpp, utf8, jsonlite, yaml, digest, stringi, cli, etc.). This suggests a binutils/assembler compatibility problem with the GCC 15.2 toolchain in this image.

- Notable context from the log
  - Many packages failed to compile due to the assembler error, not solely missing dependencies, which cascaded into later dependencies (e.g., htmltools, rmarkdown, etc.) failing to install.
  - The final blocker is the missing dependencies for dplyr, which prevents finishing the R package installation step.

- Excerpt of the failing message and exit
  - "ERROR: dependencies ‘cli’, ‘lifecycle’, ‘pillar’, ‘rlang’, ‘tibble’, ‘tidyselect’, ‘vctrs’ are not available for package ‘dplyr’"
  - "... did not complete successfully: exit code: 1"

If you need, I can suggest approaches to workaround, such as pinning a different GCC/binutils version, using prebuilt binary packages, or adjusting the Dockerfile to install critical R dependencies before compiling from source.

steps.tool_input:
{"path": "Dockerfile", "content": "FROM r-base:4.3.3

ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive

# System dependencies for building R packages
RUN apt-get update && \
    apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
      build-essential \
      libcurl4-openssl-dev \
      libssl-dev \
      libxml2-dev \
      libgit2-dev \
      libicu-dev \
      libfontconfig1-dev \
      libharfbuzz-dev \
      libfribidi-dev \
      libfreetype6-dev \
      zlib1g-dev \
      libbz2-dev \
      liblzma-dev \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

WORKDIR /usr/src/dplyr

# Copy package source
COPY . .

# Install common R build tools and dependencies (pre-install to reduce later failures)
RUN R -e 'install.packages(c("devtools","remotes"), repos="https://cloud.r-project.org")'

# Install common CRAN dependencies to help satisfy dplyr::dependencies
RUN R -e 'install.packages(c("cli","generics","lifecycle","pillar","rlang","tibble","tidyselect","vctrs"), repos="https://cloud.r-project.org")'

# Build from source
RUN R CMD INSTALL --no-multiarch --with-keep.source .

# Default shell
CMD ["bash"]
"}

verify_build_details.build_error:
Failing step and exit
- Failing command/step: RUN R CMD INSTALL --no-multiarch --with-keep.source .
- Exit code: 1
- Failing in: stage [7/7] (final install of the repo)

Exact error message (preserved)
- ERROR: dependencies ‘cli’, ‘lifecycle’, ‘pillar’, ‘rlang’, ‘tibble’, ‘tidyselect’, ‘vctrs’ are not available for package ‘dplyr’
- The overall Docker build ends with: “Dockerfile:35 >>> RUN R CMD INSTALL --no-multiarch --with-keep.source .”
- Final lines indicate cascading failures due to missing dependencies: “ERROR: dependencies ‘cli’, ‘lifecycle’, ‘pillar’, ‘rlang’, ‘tibble’, ‘tidyselect’, ‘vctrs’ are not available for package ‘dplyr’” and then the process halt.

Failing command/step and notable intermediate failures
- Step 11: RUN R CMD INSTALL --no-multiarch --with-keep.source .
- Earlier dependent steps attempted to install numerous CRAN sources from cloud, but many failed due to compilation/assembly errors (see below), causing the cascade:
  - Packages failing in install: cli, utf8, Rcpp, jsonlite, stringi, commonmark, yaml, digest, etc.
  - Recurrent cause in logs: unknown pseudo-op: `.base64` errors from assembler across multiple .s files (e.g., Rcpp, utf8, jsonlite, yaml, stringi, cli, etc.), indicating a toolchain/assembler issue.

Missing packages or files mentioned (and cascade)
- Core missing dependencies blocking dplyr: cli, lifecycle, pillar, rl ang, tibble, tidyselect, vctrs
- Cascading failures because several required dependencies could not compile/install:
  - utf8, Rcpp, jsonlite, stringi, digest, yaml, cli, systemfonts, textshaping, rlang, etc.
- Repeated mention of “unknown pseudo-op: `.base64'” in multiple packages during assembly, implying a toolchain issue rather than a simple missing file.

Version and toolchain notes
- Base image: from Dockerfile’s reference to r-base:4.3.3 (R 4.3.3, “Angel Food Cake”)
- Build environment shows Debian-side toolchain updates during apt-get (e.g., gcc-15/gcc-16, libicu-dev, openssl updates, etc.), and numerous upgrades/installations of system packages.
- The assembler errors (unknown pseudo-op “.base64”) point to a mismatch between the R package build process and the installed assembler/toolchain versions (binutils/gcc) in this image.

Actionable next steps (brief)
- Use a stabilized toolchain compatible with R 4.3.3 in this image (pin Debian/binutils/gcc versions or switch to an official R image known to build these CRAN libs reliably).
- Alternatively, build with precompiled binaries (where possible) or use a base image that ships working toolchains for CRAN packages to avoid the .base64 assembler errors.
- If sticking with source builds, address the toolchain mismatch first, then re-run the installation so that cli, rl ang, pillar, tibble, tidyselect, vctrs, etc. become available and allow dplyr to install.

steps.tool_input:
{"path": "Dockerfile", "content": "FROM r-base:4.3.3

ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive

# System dependencies for building R packages
RUN apt-get update && \
    apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
      build-essential \
      libcurl4-openssl-dev \
      libssl-dev \
      libxml2-dev \
      libgit2-dev \
      libicu-dev \
      libfontconfig1-dev \
      libharfbuzz-dev \
      libfribidi-dev \
      libfreetype6-dev \
      zlib1g-dev \
      libbz2-dev \
      liblzma-dev \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

WORKDIR /usr/src/dplyr

# Copy package source
COPY . .

# Install common R build tools (binary installs to avoid compilation issues)
RUN R -e 'install.packages(c("devtools","remotes"), repos="https://cloud.r-project.org", type="binary")'

# Install common CRAN dependencies as binaries to satisfy dplyr's deps
RUN R -e 'install.packages(c("cli","generics","lifecycle","pillar","rlang","tibble","tidyselect","vctrs"), repos="https://cloud.r-project.org", type="binary")'

# Build from source
RUN R CMD INSTALL --no-multiarch --with-keep.source .

CMD ["bash"]
"}

steps.tool_input:
{"path": "Dockerfile", "content": "FROM r-base:4.3.3

ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive

# System dependencies for building R packages
RUN apt-get update && \
    apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
      build-essential \
      libcurl4-openssl-dev \
      libssl-dev \
      libxml2-dev \
      libgit2-dev \
      libicu-dev \
      libfontconfig1-dev \
      libharfbuzz-dev \
      libfribidi-dev \
      libfreetype6-dev \
      zlib1g-dev \
      libbz2-dev \
      liblzma-dev \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

WORKDIR /usr/src/dplyr

# Copy package source
COPY . .

# Install common R build tools (fall back to source installs since binary is not guaranteed)
RUN R -e 'install.packages(c("devtools","remotes"), repos="https://cloud.r-project.org")'

# Install common CRAN dependencies to help satisfy dplyr's deps
RUN R -e 'install.packages(c("cli","generics","lifecycle","pillar","rlang","tibble","tidyselect","vctrs"), repos="https://cloud.r-project.org")'

# Build from source
RUN R CMD INSTALL --no-multiarch --with-keep.source .

CMD ["bash"]
"}

RUN R CMD INSTALL --no-multiarch --with-keep.source .

CMD ["bash"]

steps.tool_input:
{"path": "Dockerfile", "content": "FROM r-base:4.5.3

ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive

# System dependencies for building R packages
RUN apt-get update && \
    apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
      build-essential \
      libcurl4-openssl-dev \
      libssl-dev \
      libxml2-dev \
      libgit2-dev \
      libicu-dev \
      libfontconfig1-dev \
      libharfbuzz-dev \
      libfribidi-dev \
      libfreetype6-dev \
      zlib1g-dev \
      libbz2-dev \
      liblzma-dev \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

WORKDIR /usr/src/dplyr

# Copy package source
COPY . .

# Install common R build tools (to ensure dependencies can be resolved and built)
RUN R -e 'install.packages(c("devtools","remotes","cli","rlang","vctrs","glue","pillar","tibble","pkgbuild","testthat","tidyselect"), repos="https://cloud.r-project.org")'

# Build from source
RUN R CMD INSTALL --no-multiarch --with-keep.source .

CMD ["bash"]
"}
